questions.q:
A consumer survey was conducted to study the preference of people for different brands of soap. The results are given in the table below. Analyze the data and compare the preference of people for different brands of soap.

| Brand | Number of Consumers | Percentage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| A | 120 | 40% |
| B | 80 | 26.67% |
| C | 60 | 20% |
| D | 40 | 13.33% |
questions.answer: The given table shows the preference of people for different brands of soap. The data is presented in both absolute and relative (percentage) terms. The highest number of consumers prefer brand A with 120 consumers, followed by brand B with 80 consumers, brand C with 60 consumers, and brand D with the lowest number of consumers at 40. In terms of percentage, 40% of the consumers prefer brand A, 26.67% prefer brand B, 20% prefer brand C, and 13.33% prefer brand D. The bar chart or the pie chart can be used to represent this data visually.
marking_scheme: Explain the data presented in the table (1 mark)
Compare the preference of people for different brands of soap (2 marks)
Suggest a suitable method to represent this data visually (2 marks)

questions.q:
A survey was conducted among 50 students to find out their favorite subjects in school. The results are as follows:
| Subject | Number of Students | Percentage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Mathematics | 20 | 40% |
| Science | 15 | 30% |
| English | 10 | 20% |
| Others | 5 | 10% |
questions.answer: The given table shows the preference of students for different subjects in school. The data is presented in both absolute and relative (percentage) terms. The highest number of students prefer Mathematics with 20 students, followed by Science with 15 students, English with 10 students, and Others with the lowest number of students at 5. In terms of percentage, 40% of the students prefer Mathematics, 30% prefer Science, 20% prefer English, and 10% prefer Others. The bar chart or the pie chart can be used to represent this data visually. It can be observed that the majority of students prefer Mathematics and Science, while a smaller number of students prefer English and Others.
marking_scheme: Explain the data presented in the table (1 mark)
Compare the preference of students for different subjects (2 marks)
Suggest a suitable method to represent this data visually (2 marks)

questions.q:
The following table shows the marks obtained by 10 students in a class test:
| Student | Marks |
| --- | --- |
| A | 80 |
| B | 90 |
| C | 70 |
| D | 85 |
| E | 65 |
| F | 75 |
| G | 95 |
| H | 60 |
| I | 70 |
| J | 90 |
| K | 55 |
questions.topic: Presentation of Data using Graphs and Charts
questions.answer: The given table shows the marks obtained by 10 students in a class test. To analyze the data, we need to calculate the mean, median, and mode of the marks. The mean can be calculated by adding all the marks and dividing by the total number of students. The median can be found by arranging the marks in ascending order and selecting the middle value. The mode is the mark that appears most frequently. The range of the marks can be calculated by subtracting the lowest mark from the highest mark. The data can be represented visually using a histogram or a box plot.
marking_scheme: Calculate the mean of the marks (1 mark)
Find the median of the marks (1 mark)
Determine the mode of the marks (1 mark)
Calculate the range of the marks (1 mark)
Suggest a suitable method to represent this data visually (1 mark)

questions.q:
The following table shows the number of students who passed in different subjects in a school:
| Subject | Number of Students Passed | Percentage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Mathematics | 30 | 60% |
| Science | 25 | 50% |
| English | 15 | 30% |
| Others | 10 | 20% |
questions.answer: The given table shows the number of students who passed in different subjects in a school. The data is presented in both absolute and relative (percentage) terms. The highest number of students passed in Mathematics with 30 students, followed by Science with 25 students, English with 15 students, and Others with the lowest number of students at 10. In terms of percentage, 60% of the students passed in Mathematics, 50% in Science, 30% in English, and 20% in Others. The bar chart or the pie chart can be used to represent this data visually.
marking_scheme: Explain the data presented in the table (1 mark)
Compare the number of students who passed in different subjects (2 marks)
Suggest a suitable method to represent this data visually (2 marks)

questions.q:
A survey was conducted among 20 students to find out their favorite mode of transportation:
| Mode of Transportation | Number of Students | Percentage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Bus | 8 | 40% |
| Train | 6 | 30% |
| Car | 4 | 20% |
| Others | 2 | 10% |
questions.answer: The given table shows the preference of students for different modes of transportation. The data is presented in both absolute and relative (percentage) terms. The highest number of students prefer Bus with 8 students, followed by Train with 6 students, Car with 4 students, and Others with the lowest number of students at 2. In terms of percentage, 40% of the students prefer Bus, 30% prefer Train, 20% prefer Car, and 10% prefer Others. The bar chart or the pie chart can be used to represent this data visually.
marking_scheme: Explain the data presented in the table (1 mark)
Compare the preference of students for different modes of transportation (2 marks)
Suggest a suitable method to represent this data visually (2 marks)
